name: betterpathlib

metadata.name: betterpathlib
metadata.summary: A Path library that is a compatible extension to Python's built-in pathlib.Path.

documentation:
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/betterpathlib.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/betterpathlib)
[![ReadTheDocs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/betterpathlib/badge/?version=latest)](https://readthedocs.org/projects/betterpathlib)

# betterpathlib
An Path library that is an extension to Pythons built-in pathlib.Path.

```
pip install betterpathlib[all]
```

Especially useful for dealing with: 
- paths with multiple suffixes, e.g. files of the sort `archive.tar.gz`.
- paths with numerical suffixes, i.e. files of the sort `part.rar.001`.

... and in addition, adds methods for:
- Case-insensitive globbing
- Copying files and directories
- Reading JSON directly from Path object
- Downloading file from URL to destination, if the file is not already downloaded
- Atomic writes
- Random paths (with optional prefix and/or suffix)

Has no external dependencies if not installed with optional features.


## Optional features
- `download` - Requires `requests` and and allows for `or_download()`
- `similarpaths` - Requires `thefuzz` and and allows for `most_similar_paths()`

functions.name: betterpathlib.utils.bytes2human
documentation:
Return a human readble string from a given number of bytes.
Converts to kibibytes (K, Ki, or kibi depending on `symbols`),
mebibytes etc.

# Parameters

n

: Number of bytes

fmt

: String format

# Returns

str

# Examples

```pycon
>>> bytes2human("2010000")
'1.9 M'
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.__new__
documentation: The weird subclassing is in order to make sure it subclasses from PosixPath
on Posix systems (Linux, Mac) and from WindowsPath on Windows systems.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.glob_ignorecase
documentation:
Glob ignoring case

# Parameters

> pattern (str): The glob pattern to match against.

# Returns

> List\[Path\]: A list of Path objects that match the pattern, ignoring case.

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path(__file__).glob_ignorecase("*")  # doctest: +SKIP
[Path('../.git'),
 Path('../.gitignore'),
 Path('../LICENSE'),
 Path('../README.md'),
 Path('../betterpathlib'),
 Path('../setup.py')]
```

```pycon
>>> Path(__file__).glob_ignorecase("readme*")  # doctest: +SKIP
[Path('../README.md')]
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.most_similar_path
documentation:
Return the path with the most similar name. Requires module
`thefuzz` which is included when betterpathlib is installed
with the feature flag betterpathlib[similarpaths].

Parameters
---------
    recursive (bool): Whether to search recursively in subdirectories. Default is False.

Returns
-------
    Path | None: The path with the most similar name, or None if no similar paths are found.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.mtime
documentation: Last modification time, as datetime

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.modtime
documentation: Last modification time, as a nice string

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.prepend_suffix
documentation:
Prepend a suffix to the path

# Parameters

> suffix (str): The suffix to prepend.

# Returns

> Path: A new Path object with the suffix prepended.

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path('pathtools.py.xx').prepend_suffix('.new')
Path('pathtools.new.py.xx')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.has_numerical_suffix
documentation:
Check if the path has an all-digit extension.

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path("myfile.x.001").has_numerical_suffix()
True
>>> Path("myfile.x.001.feather").has_numerical_suffix()
True
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.has_primary_numerical_suffix
documentation:
Returns True if the last suffix is all digits.

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path("myfile.x.001").has_primary_numerical_suffix()
True
>>> Path("myfile.x.001.feather").has_primary_numerical_suffix()
False
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.get_numerical
documentation:
Return the first numerical extension

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path('myfile.001').get_numerical()
".001"
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.get_numerical_int
documentation:
Return the number of the first numerical extension

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path('myfile.001').get_numerical()
1
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.increase_numerical_width
documentation:
Pad the numerical suffix with `n_digs` zeroes

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path('myfile.rar.001').increase_numerical_width(4)
Path('myfile.rar.0001')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.make_numerical_suffix_nonprimary
documentation:
Shift the numerical extension as not to become the last (primary) extension

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path("myfile.x.feather.001").make_numerical_suffix_nonprimary()
Path('myfile.x.001.feather')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.make_numerical_suffix_primary
documentation:
Shift the numerical suffix to become the last (i.e. primary extension)

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path("myfile.x.001.feather").make_numerical_suffix_primary()
Path('myfile.x.feather.001')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size
documentation: Size of file in bytes

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size_kb
documentation: Size of file in kilobytes

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size_kib
documentation: Size of file in kibibytes

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size_mb
documentation: Size of file in megabytes

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size_mib
documentation: Size of file in mebibytes

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size_gb
documentation: Size of file in gigabytes

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size_gib
documentation: Size of file in gibibytes

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.size_human
documentation: Size of file in human readable format

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.disk_usage
documentation: The disk size, the amount of used and free space on the disk of this path, in bytes.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.disk_usage_human
documentation: The disk size, the amount of used and free space on the disk of this path, in human readable format (KiB, MiB, etc.).

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.move
documentation:
Recursively move a file or directory to another location. This is
similar to the Unix "mv" command. Return the file or directory's
destination.

If dst already exists but is not a directory, it may be overwritten
depending `overwrite`.

Returns
-------
The destination path

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.copy
documentation:
Copy data to the destination.

If the source path is a directory then it is recursively copied.
If the source path is a directory and `dirs_exist_ok` is False (the default) and `dst` already exists, a
`FileExistsError` is raised. If `dirs_exist_ok` is True, the copying
operation will continue if it encounters existing directories, and files
within the `dst` tree will be overwritten by corresponding files from the
`src` tree.

Returns
-------
The destination path

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.ls
documentation: Run the `ls` Linux command to list files.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.listdir
documentation: List all file in the directory

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.join
documentation:
Alias for `joinpath`.

Combine this path with one or several arguments, and return a
new path representing either a subpath (if all arguments are relative
paths) or a totally different path (if one of the arguments is
anchored).

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.with_stem
documentation:
Return a path with a new stem. Retains only the last file extension.

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path('somedir/info.py.bak').with_stem('view')
Path('somedir/view.bak')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.with_rootname
documentation:
Return a path with a new root file name. Retains all file extensions.

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path('somedir/info.py.bak').with_rootname('view')
Path('somedir/view.py.bak')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.with_parent
documentation:
Change the parent directory of given file or folder

Example:
\>>> Path('/etc/anaconda/conf.d').with_parent('/tmp')
Path('/tmp/conf.d')
\>>> Path('/usr/share/applications/emacs.desktop').with_parent("~/.local/share/applications/")
Path('~/.local/share/applications/emacs.desktop')

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.with_user
documentation:
Path for a different user home directory

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path("/home/USER_1/somefile").with_user("USER_2")  # doctest: +SKIP
Path('/home/USER_2/somefile')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.add_suffix
documentation:
Add as last suffix

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path('add_polling_info.py').add_suffix('.bak')
Path('add_polling_info.py.bak')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.append
documentation: Append to stem.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.with_suffixes
documentation:
Replace current suffix(es) with the given suffixes

Example:
\>>> Path("file.suffix1.suffix2").with_suffixes([".mkv", ".r00"])
Path('file.mkv.r00')

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.without_suffix
documentation:
Remove a suffix from path. If no suffix is specified, then the last suffix is removed.

Example:
\>>> Path('add_polling_info.py.bak.new').without_suffix('.bak')
Path('add_polling_info.py.new')

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.without_suffixes
documentation:
Remove all suffixes from from the path.
Example:
\>>> Path('add_polling_info.py.bak.new').without_suffixes()
Path('add_polling_info')

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.next_unused_path
documentation:
Return the next path with a numerical suffix that does not exist.

# Example

```pycon
>>> import tempfile
>>> dir_ = Path(tempfile.gettempdir())
>>> (dir_ / 'somefile.rar.001').touch()
>>> (dir_ / 'somefile.rar.003').touch()
>>> (dir_ / 'somefile.rar.004').touch()
>>> (dir_ / 'somefile.rar.001').next_unused_path()
Path('/tmp/somefile.rar.002')
>>> (dir_ / 'somefile.rar.003').next_unused_path()
Path('/tmp/somefile.rar.005')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.last_numerical_path
documentation:
Returns the last/highest numerical path

# Example

```pycon
>>> import tempfile
>>> dir_ = Path(tempfile.gettempdir())
>>> p1 = dir_ / 'somefile.rar.001'
>>> (dir_ / 'somefile.rar.004').touch()
>>> p1.last_numerical_path()
Path('/tmp/somefile.rar.004')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.or_download
documentation:
Download file atomically if it doesn't exist locally.
A progress bar is shown if `progress_bar` is True.
If the Path is a directory, then the file_name is inferred from the URL.
The Path for the destination file is returned.

Additional keyword-arguments are passed to `requests.get`

Requires `requests` and `tqdm`, if progress_bar is True.
These two modules are included with the feature flag
betterpath[dowwnload]

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.read_json
documentation: Read a JSON file. Additional keyword-arguments will be passed to json.load

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.write_json
documentation: Write JSON file. Additional keyword-arguments will be passed to json.dump

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.read_pickle
documentation: Read the pickled representation of the object.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.write_pickle
documentation: Write the pickled representation of the object obj to the path. Additional keyword-arguments will be passed to pickle.dump

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.atomic_write
documentation:
Write data atomically to avoid partial writes.

Args:
    data: Content to write
    mode: File open mode ('w' for text, 'wb' for binary)
    **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to open()

Raises:
    OSError: If write fails

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.atomic_write_with
documentation:
Write data atomically with the given function to avoid partial writes.

Args:
    write_fn: A callable taking a Path in string form for writing data
    **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to write_fn

Returns:
    The result from write_fn

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.is_same_file
documentation: Check if two paths point to the same file (following symlinks).

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.is_relative_to_home
documentation: Check if path is under user's home directory.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.chdir
documentation:
Change the current working directory to the specified path.

On some platforms, path may also be specified as an open file descriptor.
If this functionality is unavailable, using it raises an exception.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.mkdir_
documentation: Create a new directory at this given path. Returns itself.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.tempdir
documentation: Returns the system's temporary directory

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.mkdtemp
documentation:
User-callable function to create and return a unique temporary
directory.  The return value is the pathname of the directory.

Arguments are as for mkstemp, except that the 'text' argument is
not accepted.

The directory is readable, writable, and searchable only by the
creating user.

Caller is responsible for deleting the directory when done with it.

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.random_path
documentation:
Return a random, unused path. If `dir` is not given, then the path
will be in a temporary directory.

# Example

```pycon
>>> Path.random_path("cat_image-", ".png")  # doctest: +SKIP
Path('/tmp/cat_image-m3xx9q0q.png')
```

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.glob_cwd
documentation: Glob the current working directory

methods.name: betterpathlib.main.Path.git_root
documentation:
Find the Path of the file of the calling function, and traverse upwards the directory tree
until a '.git' directory is found. This is usually the project root of the calling function.
